S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-19 5:13 p.m.
Just a heads-up – I’ve been tinkering with something called MeshRank It’s basically a way of giving a bit more visibility into what the mesh is actually doing, without changing how MeshCORE works. No internet routing, no messing with RF, nothing in the message path. It just listens, observes, and feeds back what already happened. Why I built it: • New repeaters often get switched off because it’s hard to tell if they’re helping • People end up spamming test messages just to see if anything’s alive • When stuff doesn’t propagate, there’s no clue why MeshRank gives things like delivery confidence, shows which repeaters are contributing, and highlights where messages are being seen but not going any further. Observers are totally passive — they don’t transmit or interfere in any way. If MeshRank fell over tomorrow, the mesh would carry on exactly the same. I’m looking for a few folks (UK first) who wouldn’t mind running an observer and helping build some early coverage — high ground / urban spots especially useful. If you fancy a look: https://meshrank.net Happy to answer questions or take criticism
1

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-01-23 8:14 p.m.
Q-codes, from back in the morse days, which have made their way into standard ham parlance. QRT = I will stop transmitting, i.e. go offline. QRO is high power, etc etc... take a look at this pdf https://www.arrl.org/files/file/Get%20on%20the%20Air/Comm%20w%20Other%20Hams-Q%20Signals.pdf (edited)
8:17 p.m.
Also get used in aviation still to some extent (QNH = barometric pressure at field)
Jason G5RKT IO90hw
And you, until next time

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-01-30 1:49 a.m.
Morning Peter. I have spent some time attempting to build some super J poles - yep. Incredibly difficult to get right, I've made three now and none of them are on freq - too many elements to tune in one go! The single ele J pole was trivial (just done with some twin & earth core) but trying to get the delay lines and extra elements trimmed correctly has been an excercise in annoyance
1:52 a.m.
All good tho. Going to do some experiments up at our Winchester site this evening / this weekend to see what we can get up. Meshrank is also suggesting that the So'ton box (67) could do with better receive performance, so we're going to install a higher gain colinear on it at some point, initial results are encouraging though.
1:54 a.m.
Furthermore we're planning to get another repeater up in Portswood, as testing suggests that the 67 box is a bit of a stretch for that face of the Itchen valley... still a hit and miss for me at home to get into IW SD2 (27) or So'ton (67) so another box is in order. Good times!

Hi Everyone. I'm new to the mesh. My immediate area seems to have no coverage I can see with the cheap companion device I bought. I think I'm going to need to put in my own repeater. The two nearest repeaters I can see on the mesh map are "Lando Loft Repeater" and "M34A Repeater" but I can't see them on letsmesh. What's the best way to determine if they are connected to the wider mesh or if they're an island please?

Furthermore we're planning to get another repeater up in Portswood, as testing suggests that the 67 box is a bit of a stretch for that face of the Itchen valley... still a hit and miss for me at home to get into IW SD2 (27) or So'ton (67) so another box is in order. Good times!
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-01-30 1:37 p.m.
Hey Jason, I have been toying with something stealth Meon Valley area or a bit toward Winchester (Cheesefoot) but I'll hang on now. Have you had a look at some traces from 67 to other key sites? I decided it would be too hard to try and make a super J based on how fiddly the single one is. Pleased I never tried now. I've got an aliexress 5.5, Paradar 6.5 and McGill 6dB. My simple J is 3~6dB better to Butser and Chillerton. A bit disappointed I wasted so much money but hey!
Jason G5RKT IO90hw
Morning Peter. I have spent some time attempting to build some super J poles - yep. Incredibly difficult to get right, I've made three now and none of them are on freq - too many elements to tune in one go! The single ele J pole was trivial (just done with some twin & earth core) but trying to get the delay lines and extra elements trimmed correctly has been an excercise in annoyance
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-01-30 4:37 p.m.
Replacement Portsdown node FW updated. Will be deployed in the next couple of days. Once I've got the other once back, I'll be looking to find a spot for it... (edited)

Hey Peter! Interesting stuff, I wish I was in a better position to run some traces. Currently for me getting into IW SD2 (27) s a bit hit-and-miss, and I can only occasionally hit 67 too. 6:00 a.m.
Interesting stuff with the antennas. My J-pole seems to perform about the same as the 868 whip when I ping IW SD2, the 868 whip is a Nagoya-like clone which I've trimmed down to get in on freq. For the 67 repeater we've ordered the biggest Paradar colinear which will, if the numbers are to be believed, give us another ~3dB. Let's see eh! I have also got an el-cheapo 868 5ele beam on the way from Aliexpress which I'm hoping will allow me more success from home.. but we just need more repeaters around town.

i currently have two available but i dont really know a huge amount about antennas. https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-5dbi-indoor-whip https://thepihut.com/products/lora-antenna-with-pigtail-868mhz-black i'm open to guidance on whats going to achieve a good omnidirectional coverage
868Mhz 5dBi whip antenna with SMA + RP-SMA connector for LoRa, Mesh...
Free shipping via Royal Mail 48 to UK addresses The smallest of our range, our 5dBi indoor antennas are an enhanced tuned dipole design from brass tube, producing an extremely efficient response at the chosen frequency. The "old-fashioned" design is more efficient, less affected by nearby objects and interference and a
LoRa Antenna with Pigtail - 868MHz Black
This is a 170mm black 868MHz 50ohm antenna kit tested and recommended by iLabs for use with their LoRa products, such as the Challenger RP2040 LoRa. They can also be used with any other LoRa board requiring an 868MHz U.FL antenna solution.

@G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT have you got any power draw or battery level statistics from that nRF52 node?
ｖｏｉｄ
@G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT have you got any power draw or battery level statistics from that nRF52 node?
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-03 11:21 a.m.
Very little power draw on the nRF modules. around 8mA idle apparently. I have 9000mAh. Battery has not gone below 85% and normally chares to 95 % as soon as there is any sun.
ｖｏｉｄ 2026-02-03 11:26 a.m.
damn thats good, i've bought a 2W panel and im trying to get a baseline of how many cells/mah ill need
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-03 11:33 a.m.
2w panel might be pushing it in the winter. Just some back of an envelope sums. Say you've got 5000mAh 50% DoC is 2500. at 8mA (no tx) that'll give you 312 hours or 13 days. Now lets add some TX time in.... I'd say and just a guess divide by 2 = 6 days with 5000mAh and no charge at all.
pir 2026-02-03 11:35 a.m.
People seem to be going for 10W as a good amount
11:35 a.m.

11:35 a.m.
For a higher efficiency board like a T114 or an nRF

S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-05 4:33 a.m.
Morning all — a few updates to MeshRank.net: Heltec V4 can now run as a combined Observer + Repeater (beta). An NTP time sync has been added once Wi-Fi connects, so the device always has the correct date/time after reboot. This also includes the Heltec V4 SX1262 / GC1109 RX fix. As this is beta, please don’t update the repeater that’s bolted to your roof just yet The MQTT feed is now open for anyone already using meshcoretomqtt (or similar) and wanting to mirror their packet stream into MeshRank. Advanced packet routing maps are nearly finished. These are based on mutual neighbour connections and can infer likely paths where there are gaps in the observed route. I’m also finalising MeshLive — a real-time visual view of the mesh, including country-wide noise floor, live routes, and packets per minute. This will be released to observers first.
3
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-05 5:14 a.m.
Solent_BOT is mine. It answers rig checks (ping, test, path) in #test and reports to meshrank.net and analyzer.letsmesh.net (PME)
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-05 6:11 a.m.
You requested a live map showing last 60 minutes of all packet data overlaid on a map showing the full mesh interconnection. No problem it’s live now on MeshRank.net under the MeshRank page. Enjoy

You requested a live map showing last 60 minutes of all packet data overlaid on a map showing the full mesh interconnection. No problem it’s live now on MeshRank.net under the MeshRank page. Enjoy

Improve GC1109 FEM pin handling for Heltec v4 and Tracker v2 by wee...
Improves the GC1109 FEM pin management for Heltec v4 and Heltec Tracker v2. These are correctness and robustness improvements to how GPIO46 (CPS strapping pin) and deep sleep pins are handled - not...
mikebennett0612
I think the V4s are suffering from this : https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1249 - going to relegate mine to home base duties!
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-06 5:07 p.m.
I fixed this issue when I was building the MeshRank repeater firmware for the Heltec V4. I’ve also added NTP lookup so once the repeater has connected to the internet it will also update the time. Fortunately it also includes MQTT so it will also become a MeshRank.net observer. No additional boxes. Simple flash, then add WiFi and MQTT key and you’re running. It’s beta at the moment but maybe worth a try?

linickx 2026-02-10 6:09 a.m.
Hello. Roughly how long does an Everyday Carry Device need to sit (outside?) to pick something up? I don't have a repeater. Looking at the maps posted above, I thought I might be in range, but so far nothing. Like for example, if I sit at a coffee shop in Alton, would that work? TIA!
linickx
Hello. Roughly how long does an Everyday Carry Device need to sit (outside?) to pick something up? I don't have a repeater. Looking at the maps posted above, I thought I might be in range, but so far nothing. Like for example, if I sit at a coffee shop in Alton, would that work? TIA!
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-10 6:12 a.m.
You need to be pretty close, within a few miles of a repeater. I would imagine in Alton you'll not hear much at all without a dedicated antenna up high and in the clear.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
You need to be pretty close, within a few miles of a repeater. I would imagine in Alton you'll not hear much at all without a dedicated antenna up high and in the clear.
linickx 2026-02-10 6:13 a.m.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-10 6:17 a.m.
maybe have a wonder up to the Golden Pot area. That would be the best area with any chance of hearing a repeater. That part of Hants isn't really well covered yet. Have a look at the map here. https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/map?lat=51.12174&long=-0.78786&zoom=9

is portsmouthBOT meant to be spamming Butser south with trafic like this?
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-13 5:31 p.m.
One of the biggest challenges with MeshCore is the dreaded Hashbyte issue. Repeaters identify themselves with a 2 digit code. It actually doesn’t affect the mesh. But makes it difficult to work out exactly which route a message has taken. I’ve added a repeater ID clashes page to the site and added a personalised review for each repeater on the repeater rank. So if you have a close neighbour sharing the same code it helps you pick a safe option. You can then log into your repeater and update the code in Tools. Check it out on Meshrank.net Hope it helps. (edited)
2
ｖｏｉｄ
is portsmouthBOT meant to be spamming Butser south with trafic like this?
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-13 5:39 p.m.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
I was pinging it while moving a beam around to find the spot with the strongest signal.
ｖｏｉｄ 2026-02-13 5:45 p.m.
ahh okay
ｖｏｉｄ
ahh okay
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-13 5:49 p.m.
The bot was only reporting the RX of a trace rather than actually transmitting anything. It reports to the analyzser and meshrank. I was pinging with my personal node.
ｖｏｉｄ
is portsmouthBOT meant to be spamming Butser south with trafic like this?
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-13 6:46 p.m.
MeshRank.net sees around 60,000 unique packets a day. Only 4,000 of those are messages. Personally I think we should embrace all messages. I don’t see any evidence of bots swamping the network. More messages will ultimately lead to more users joining. Flood adverts are the real problem. (edited)
ｖｏｉｄ 2026-02-13 6:47 p.m.
understood, i'm still new to core so idk whats good and bad traffic
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-13 6:48 p.m.
We’re all learning. That’s what’s great about it. When I joined public was empty. It’s great to see it coming alive
S7uart G5AIL
MeshRank.net sees around 60,000 unique packets a day. Only 4,000 of those are messages. Personally I think we should embrace all messages. I don’t see any evidence of bots swamping the network. More messages will ultimately lead to more users joining. Flood adverts are the real problem. (edited)
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-13 6:49 p.m.
Is that from people overusing he flood adverts button, or just the nature of using flood messages?
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-13 6:53 p.m.
I’m not 100% sure. I’ve tried to discourage it in the repeater rankings. A flood advert isn’t required for the mesh to operate. It’s more for user confidence. Which is partly why I built the site. I just feel that an advert takes up a lot of network utility and satisfies only 1 person. Whereas a message takes up less and potentially satisfies many.
6:55 p.m.
I was showing my parents tonight. They were flicking through the messages thinking this is really busy. Now they want a repeater in their loft. If I showed them it full of adverts they wouldn’t have been so keen
1
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-13 6:57 p.m.
Yep agreed - I guess one every now and again is okay, but folks must be flood adverting all the time. It's amazing looking at our box (So'ton West 67), it's been up three weeks and in that time has been transmitting for 15 hours and receiveing for 2 days! 270 rx, 68k tx... mental (edited)
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-13 6:57 p.m.
I built this to visualise the mesh in realtime You’re welcome to take a look. It’s a visual of the mesh messages almost live. When you see it, you’ll see it’s not that busy. (edited)

7:07 p.m.

Jason G5RKT IO90hw
Is that from people overusing he flood adverts button, or just the nature of using flood messages?
pir 2026-02-14 4:23 a.m.
It's mostly the time between automatic adverts and the power on adverts. There have been discussions about disabling the power on adverts (especially since one node in a boot loop can produce a lot of traffic like that) and increasing the default interval on flood adverts. I set my flood advert numbers much higher, nearer 48h
pir
It's mostly the time between automatic adverts and the power on adverts. There have been discussions about disabling the power on adverts (especially since one node in a boot loop can produce a lot of traffic like that) and increasing the default interval on flood adverts. I set my flood advert numbers much higher, nearer 48h
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-14 6:48 a.m.
Ah yes the power-on adverts, that would make a huge difference. I just checked and our box is set to the default 24hrs so I just upped it to 48hrs, good call on that.
6:49 a.m.
Hey @G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT, Banger and I are gonna get up on site somewhere around 1430 today to do some tinkering with the Soton West box, do you fancy doing some traces and whatnot? We have two experiments to do, swap the antenna and note the change in performance, then up the TX power and note it again. Meshrank suggests we're hearing, but not being heard by a few nodes so we could definitely QRO

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-14 11:56 a.m.

1
11:56 a.m.
Paradar 11.5dBi antenna looks like this - pretty wide tuning (edited)
11:59 a.m.
And our RF situation on site is pretty exciting! Marker 2 is sat on a constant signal at 867.3MHz, very close to 869.6MHz.. and all the "stuff" around us is very loud indeed.
12:00 p.m.
Noise floor of the receiver with a dummy load attached is -114dBm, with the antenna attached it's -95dBm. Good news is we're not suffering from conducted interference, it's all coming down the antenna so filtering is gonna help an awful lot! With a bit of twiddling we should be able to knock down the signal at 867.3 by a good 30dB and keep our channel with minimal insertion loss
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
after
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-14 12:02 p.m.
Oh wow.. that's much better!!

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-15 8:31 a.m.
Me too, 18dB return loss on channel is just fine. It's this badboy https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-tuned-antenna-for-hnt-11-5dbi
868Mhz tuned 11.5dBi antenna for HNT & LoRa
Extend the range of your Helium hotspot with our high quality ultra-low-SWR tuned LoRa antenna, for outdoor use. Designed in the United Kingdom from aviation-grade components, and provides excellent range for HNT (Helium) hotspots as well as for aviation (FLARM or OGN) applications. Compatible with all Helium miners.

Interesting stuff; with the old Soton WEST antenna the noise floor was around -95 and the last SNR (probably my inbound connection) was reasonably consistent at -10, but now the noise floor is slightly lower at around -98 and the last SNR is around -4, so the new antenna is (maybe) rejecting the interference a little better and hearing the mesh more clearly (average of a bunch of readings)

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-17 7:38 a.m.
Just a heads up for Solent area people. Butser Hill our main trunk route out to the wider mesh was swapped on Sunday evening under the cover of dark. Sadly the link quality has dropped massively I can only just get it from home now it's gone from +6 to -8. Only seeing a small fraction of traffic now on my observer node that reports to Meshrank and the analyzer. We really need another route to RG17 or something else along the Berkshire downs. Any suggestions? (edited)

mikebennett0612 2026-02-18 3:27 p.m.
My one advice - if you can get it out of the loft to outside - safely - go for it. I had mine in my loft for two months with no messages - as soon as it is outside (same height) - loads of signal. If you can do it - its the best upgrade!

mikebennett0612 2026-02-21 6:28 a.m.
Been a bit quiet here lately!!! Update on my location. V4 has been tested on a temp pole and am happy it works. Lots of traffic recieved, but nothing out yet to the main mesh. Driving around shows a decent 6km coverage. Next step is to get the scaffolding up so I can mount it on my TV Antenna (replace my old wideband SDR antenna). Will also be putting a new loft node (V4 - Gizont 45cm) up in KT24 over the next couple of weeks. Every little helps. Also going wardriving this afternoon - so mobile client will be getting up nice and high on the north downs for a hour or so of comms.
mikebennett0612
Been a bit quiet here lately!!! Update on my location. V4 has been tested on a temp pole and am happy it works. Lots of traffic recieved, but nothing out yet to the main mesh. Driving around shows a decent 6km coverage. Next step is to get the scaffolding up so I can mount it on my TV Antenna (replace my old wideband SDR antenna). G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-21 6:42 a.m.

Luke Edwards 2026-02-21 1:28 p.m.
In terms of updating a device up high, can you do that via a companion device, or do you have to climb up and get the device? Would an alternative be to run some wire from the device inside the loft via a short cable to the device?
mikebennett0612 2026-02-21 1:33 p.m.
I used 20m of 12v automotive cable, and a 12v Buck converter to USBC in my case. Advantages are that you can make the cable run as long as you want, and use random DC power supplies. Updates over Wifi easy. (log on to repeater, serial , then "start ota" Creates a Wifi netowrk to join and then update.. Easy to reboot from the PSU.
Luke Edwards 2026-02-21 1:35 p.m.
Ah interesting thanks for the information. We’re getting our roof replaced in a few weeks so might give me an easy installation
mikebennett0612 2026-02-21 1:38 p.m.
The above is assuming you are using a wifi capable device (Heltek) other ones (tat I have not used) can be done by BLE apparently!
Luke Edwards 2026-02-21 1:38 p.m.
Yep heltek v4

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
In other news. I had a go at mesh war driving this afternoon and put Portsmouth on the map!
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-23 6:27 a.m.
whats your set up for war driving do you just use a companion or do you use a mobile repeater too and set the app to ignore the mobile repeater? I did a long drive from MK to IOW on Friday and logged it, didnt pick up many rpeeaters I assume due to the glass in the car refelcting the RF.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
whats your set up for war driving do you just use a companion or do you use a mobile repeater too and set the app to ignore the mobile repeater? I did a long drive from MK to IOW on Friday and logged it, didnt pick up many rpeeaters I assume due to the glass in the car refelcting the RF.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-23 6:32 a.m.
Only works with a companion to discover & ping neighbours. Mobile repeater would be a bad idea! I had had a Seeed Xiao S3 with a tiny mag mount on the roof of the car. This is the type of antenna. I cut it down for 869 Mhz.
1
6:32 a.m.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
Only works with a companion to discover & ping neighbours. Mobile repeater would be a bad idea! I had had a Seeed Xiao S3 with a tiny mag mount on the roof of the car. This is the type of antenna. I cut it down for 869 Mhz.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-24 3:03 p.m.

Perhaps even fewer hops would drive the network to have fewer, better sited repeaters / longer links, and make the poorly siited repeaters less attractive to fire up

Luke Edwards 2026-02-25 12:13 p.m.

pir
What are you using for the monitoring/graphing? Something I haven't looked into yet.
mikebennett0612 2026-02-25 12:34 p.m.
Ive got a dedicated V3 (usb) plugged into my home assistant server, and then running homeassitant_ha on that. It logs into my repeater every 10 mins and gets stats.... Quite useful to see! If i lower my mast by 2m to 8m for instance, my incoming message rate halves...Also got it monitoring my car node, so tells me when i need to charge it! Also good for Pingbots (on my own channel) to get home information (temperature etc) when I am out and can hit my repeater!

mikebennett0612 2026-02-25 4:10 p.m.
I have two. They were useless until the latest version 1.13 came along (i erased both and reflashed) - after that they actually worked as expected, and comparing against my v3 - I heard more, and wardriving i seem to get out a bit further. All anecodatal of course. Are you running 1.13???
4:11 p.m.
Also - both of mine seemed to flash with a TX setting of 10 for some reason - worth checking you are at 20 or 22....
Luke Edwards 2026-02-25 4:12 p.m.
Oh what does that do? Up the power ?
mikebennett0612 2026-02-25 4:13 p.m.
I stand to be corrected but - 22 is the max meshcore uses - the V4 then has a further RF amp, which takes it up to around 28db... about 0.5 watt - much more than a V3 (thats the only reason I got them!!!)
Luke Edwards 2026-02-25 4:13 p.m.
Brb checking settings
mikebennett0612 2026-02-25 4:13 p.m.
Mine are set both at 22 at the moment for testing.. (repeater & mobile client) - i have tinkered with 18 & 20 - im thinking 22 is the best if you have no DC power worries...

mikebennett0612 2026-02-25 4:54 p.m.

pir 2026-02-25 4:54 p.m.
Though this quote probably means that it isn't needed anymore: The problem isn't turning off the LNA, the problem was the LNA wasn't being turned on after transmitting (and RX ending up in the TX path ALWAYS) The LNA doesn't need to be turned off - it needs to be switched INTO the RF path. That's what PA_TX_EN controls. When LOW, the switch routes through LNA. The fix ensures PA_TX_EN is LOW during RX.
1
4:55 p.m.
https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1249#issuecomment-3684796041
pir
Though this quote probably means that it isn't needed anymore: The problem isn't turning off the LNA, the problem was the LNA wasn't being turned on after transmitting (and RX ending up in the TX path ALWAYS) The LNA doesn't need to be turned off - it needs to be switched INTO the RF path. That's what PA_TX_EN controls. When LOW, the switch routes through LNA. The fix ensures PA_TX_EN is LOW during RX.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-25 4:56 p.m.
Riiiiiight yep. Still getting up to speed, or as much as my muggle brain can handle anyway
Jason G5RKT IO90hw
It's a brilliant thread! This bit is most enlightening https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1249#issuecomment-3753206206
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-25 5:02 p.m.
Cool. From the comment above then there are three things here:
  • Register patch to fix LNA switching into RX path (good, proper fix)
  • GPIO wire-patch to force the above behaviour (temp workaround, superseeded by the register patch)
  • LNA gain "boosting" (comes with all the usual caveats of using more LNA gain) Thanks folks for steering me through the wild forests of github pulls, it's all rather indigestible at first glance
(edited)
1
Jason G5RKT IO90hw
Cool. From the comment above then there are three things here:
  • Register patch to fix LNA switching into RX path (good, proper fix)
  • GPIO wire-patch to force the above behaviour (temp workaround, superseeded by the register patch)
  • LNA gain "boosting" (comes with all the usual caveats of using more LNA gain) Thanks folks for steering me through the wild forests of github pulls, it's all rather indigestible at first glance
(edited)
Bryan#1364 (CATM) 2026-02-26 4:20 a.m.
I wish this had been handled better and was clear about what fixes had been rolled out. I'm not disputing that maybe there have been bugs found in the firmware but much of findings do not stack up with real life. There is a basic problem of taking a wideband chip with no filtering and adding an LNA and connecting it to a high gain antenna in our busy environment. Overload and rx blocking is almost inevitable. Many people found a complete cure of deafness was to fit a cavity filter or in one case to simply remove the LNA with a wire bridge. If the issue was down to these FW fixes these two sensible fixes for an overloaded receiver would not have fixed the issue so just bare in mind it's there's a lot more involved. No-one has mentioned the G2 which suffers the same or worse deafness issues due to an even higher gain LNA not seen anyone rolling out these FW fixes for the G2
Bryan#1364 (CATM)
I wish this had been handled better and was clear about what fixes had been rolled out. I'm not disputing that maybe there have been bugs found in the firmware but much of findings do not stack up with real life. There is a basic problem of taking a wideband chip with no filtering and adding an LNA and connecting it to a high gain antenna in our busy environment. Overload and rx blocking is almost inevitable. Many people found a complete cure of deafness was to fit a cavity filter or in one case to simply remove the LNA with a wire bridge. If the issue was down to these FW fixes these two sensible fixes for an overloaded receiver would not have fixed the issue so just bare in mind it's there's a lot more involved. No-one has mentioned the G2 which suffers the same or worse deafness issues due to an even higher gain LNA not seen anyone rolling out these FW fixes for the G2
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-26 4:29 a.m.
It's all good Bryan, I'm new round these parts have entirely failed to stay on top of progress, so that's on me. I fully understand/agree with issues surrounding wide front-end gain, indeed our well-sited meshcore repeater site gets hammered by all sorts of signals right across UHF proper and the only solution to that is of course filtering.. which is always the solution to any busy RF environment anyway. As we have had to do with our ham repeater / data stations, all of which are on busy sites. Comes with the territory! I admire the efforts of everyone who has worked through the issue at a firmware level, and all the folks doing the on-air experiments. Thanks for your message!! 🫡
Bryan#1364 (CATM)
I wish this had been handled better and was clear about what fixes had been rolled out. I'm not disputing that maybe there have been bugs found in the firmware but much of findings do not stack up with real life. There is a basic problem of taking a wideband chip with no filtering and adding an LNA and connecting it to a high gain antenna in our busy environment. Overload and rx blocking is almost inevitable. Many people found a complete cure of deafness was to fit a cavity filter or in one case to simply remove the LNA with a wire bridge. If the issue was down to these FW fixes these two sensible fixes for an overloaded receiver would not have fixed the issue so just bare in mind it's there's a lot more involved. No-one has mentioned the G2 which suffers the same or worse deafness issues due to an even higher gain LNA not seen anyone rolling out these FW fixes for the G2

pir 2026-02-27 2:51 a.m.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-02-27 9:26 a.m.
The P1 has for some bizarre reason an RP-SMA so keep that in mind when ordering your bits to make up the cable.
pir 2026-02-27 10:19 a.m.
and you'll want a cable to N female bulkhead connector to mount the antenna. I swapped out the RP-SMA pigtail for a normal SMA one so it didn't bite me. I'd recommend sealing up all the antenna connections too. Layer of electrical tape, layer of self-amalgamating tape then another layer of electrical tape. You can buy the tape on ebaaaay

Papfox 2026-02-27 5:46 p.m.
I'm going to be conpletely reflashing my repeater down to the bare metal tomorrow. I need to install the OTAFIX bootloader. I assume this will change its key. Is there a way to back up the current key so it doesn't change across the update?
Papfox
I'm going to be conpletely reflashing my repeater down to the bare metal tomorrow. I need to install the OTAFIX bootloader. I assume this will change its key. Is there a way to back up the current key so it doesn't change across the update?
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-28 3:34 a.m.
Hey!
3:36 a.m.
Yes, you can get the private key by using the USB console; run the command get prv.key - that'll spit it out and then you can use set prv.key on the new board
1
Luke Edwards 2026-02-28 6:25 a.m.
If I was to get something like this to route the antenna through, would it weaken the signal? https://paradar.co.uk/products/ultra-low-loss-x-400-cable-n-male-to-rp-sma-male-15m
Ultra-low-loss X-400 cable, N-male to RP-SMA male, 15m
LMR-400 cable is about the best cable you can buy for use with frequencies between 500Mhz and 3Ghz. It's the bees knees! The ultra low attenuation of 1.30dB per metre between DC and 3GHz means that almost no signal is lost in the cable. We recommend this cable for Helium / HNT mining (and other 868Mhz applications!).
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-28 6:27 a.m.
It will always attentuate to some extent, here's the datasheet https://timesmicrowave.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/lmr-400-datasheet-1.pdf Looking at 12.8dB/100m, so for 15m about 2dB signal loss
Luke Edwards 2026-02-28 6:28 a.m.
Ah cool thanks. Still new to this!
1
6:29 a.m.
I just dread building something and installing it and then not being able to repair device once it’s on the roof. At least the antenna is the only bit I can’t access easily!
6:31 a.m.
I’m possibly losing more signal going through the roof at the moment, especially as the leaky roof is getting redone and will likely have more insulation shortly
Luke Edwards
I’m possibly losing more signal going through the roof at the moment, especially as the leaky roof is getting redone and will likely have more insulation shortly
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-28 6:32 a.m.
Oh yeah almost certainly, no idea what the attentuation of wet tiles at 900MHz is but I bet it's more than 2dB! Plus if they put in that Cellotex / foil lined stuff then it'll be deaf as a post
Luke Edwards 2026-02-28 6:33 a.m.
Right I’m going to get it ordered. The roofers said they will remove and fit anything for cups of tea
Luke Edwards
I just dread building something and installing it and then not being able to repair device once it’s on the roof. At least the antenna is the only bit I can’t access easily!
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-28 6:36 a.m.
Legends. Can I recommend going to town waterproofing the connection between the antenna and the coax? A dab of silicone grease on the female-N side, then once its attached a continuous spiral of leccy tape (3M Temflex ideally) to completely encapsulate everything all the way down to the coax, then on top of that a layer of self-amalgamating tape to protect the PVC
1
Luke Edwards 2026-02-28 6:36 a.m.
Thanks very much for the info and yes I plan on making it extremely waterproof!
Luke Edwards

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-02-28 6:37 a.m.
Excellent. Go bananas!!

This is an ideal starter kit for any Meshtastic and LoRa/LoRaWAN projects! The kit combines the XIAO ESP32S3 microcontroller with the Wio-SX1262 LoRa module to add Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE), and LoRa, which opens up the possibilities to what you can create next! Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-01 5:08 p.m.
£99 from this place for something similar, apparently https://shop.airframes.io/products/lora-915mhz-filter
LoRa 4MHz-wide Cavity Filter
A premium LoRa 900MHz-related band cavity filter that provides low-loss filtering of 900-915 MHz LoRa signals between an antenna and a LoRa device. Increases signal SNR, reduces out of band harmonics, and allows for the weaker and distant nodes to be heard. The Airframes LoRa Cavity Filter is Ideal for Meshtastic, Mesh
5:10 p.m.
Maybe. Says "Tunable 890-960", bit on the short side perhaps

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-01 5:13 p.m.
Yeah and disassembling those things I can imagine is a complete no-go. I found this on a Danish site, no price tho - and if you have to ask the price, well... https://ws.dk/en/868-mhz-50-watts-band-pass-filter/ Probably around the usual Procom prices I imagine
868 MHz Band Pass Filter - WS Kommunikation
868 MHz 50 Watts Band Pass Filter The FP-868 is a 3-cavity band-pass filter using full-length quarter-wave resonators. The filter can be tuned within the ISM band, 865-870 MHz band. Careful design and choice of materials ensure reliable operation over a wide temperature range

I notice Meshrank is marking me down a little because my repeater is advertising twice a day. Is this an issue? What is the etiquette for adverts?
Luke Edwards 2026-03-02 3:25 a.m.
Every 12 hours is the standard I thought?!
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-02 3:27 a.m.
Our box does zero hop ads every 4hrs and floods every 3 days.. the majority of mesh traffic is flood ads, so I think the idea is that less is more
2
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-02 5:20 a.m.
@M0LIH - Ian - Gosport Is your BBS up and running ?
Papfox
I notice Meshrank is marking me down a little because my repeater is advertising twice a day. Is this an issue? What is the etiquette for adverts?
pir 2026-03-02 5:43 a.m.
Adverts are a non-trivial proportion of the network traffic and the mesh has very limited bandwidth. I would suggest 24h as the minimum time for adverts, preferably closer to 48h. They aren't needed to make the mesh work.

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-02 2:26 p.m.
I been tinkering too this evening. @G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT I reckon that filter has made a difference, HBI was previously hearing Portsdown about +5, on average, maybe, and I just ran a whole bunch of traces and it seems it's hearing it around +9 pretty often, maybe.. difficult to do super reliable experiments but it "feels" better. Home -> HBI averaged about -7, now I'm averaging more like -4.. have to keep a close eye
2:26 p.m.
Specan traces - HBI antenna, no filter
2:26 p.m.
HBI antenna with SAW filter, something like 40dB rejection out-of-band, perhaps 2.5dB loss in-band, ties up with the S21 measurement of the filter (edited)
2:27 p.m.
Super close-in sweep showing the other 868 stuff it can hear, pretty loud at -65dBm
2:30 p.m.
Noise floor has dropped from -95 / -98 to -102 / -104. Ish.

djembejim 2026-03-02 5:02 p.m.

ｖｏｉｄ 2026-03-02 5:02 p.m.
2 watt
2W Solar Panel 80mm x 180mm
This 2W solar panel is made of single-crystal material that performs solar energy transformation at 17% efficiency! It has a clear epoxy resin surface and sturdy hard-board back, which makes it perfect for outdoor environments.
djembejim 2026-03-02 5:03 p.m.
That can only be 100ma full sun???
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-02 5:12 p.m.
I have been through some fairly crappy antennas over the last month or so for my home repeater. Listened to the various people saying to get something from an Aliexpress vendor called Gizont, made the mistake of assuming this would mean all the products sold by that vendor were good.
5:13 p.m.
Fortunately pfennet was able to lend me a DIYMall antenna which he'd had success with, but which he no longer needed for a while. That seemed to be the most reliable I'd used, so it's been the daily drive for a couple of weeks and performs decently (considering the repeater is currently in an upstairs window).
5:15 p.m.
For the record, the Gizont 200mm and 400mm antennas I tested seemed pretty crap. Then I read more reviews and articles and the general consensus appeared to be that their 170mm antennas were better, because of magic antenna stuff I don't entirely understand. Given those are £5, I though sod it an ordered one along with some other stuff, which just arrived.
5:16 p.m.
That one DOES appear to actually be half decent!
djembejim
That can only be 100ma full sun???
ｖｏｉｄ 2026-03-02 5:19 p.m.
10v/600mA peak during full sun, into a Adafruit bq25185 solar/li-on charger that is stepping voltage down to 3v for the Xiao. i think the maths should be okay, the weather is not. the battery seems to be sitting at 3.2v so i'll charge it up and see what happens
RichKS (TacticalBacon)
That one DOES appear to actually be half decent!
djembejim 2026-03-02 5:19 p.m.
Gizont N-Type have all tested good for me. Did get them months ago so maybe a different batch but they’ve all tested good on the vna..
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-02 5:20 p.m.
The DIYMall antenna was giving me traces to Soton West HBI Omni around 6/-6 and this new one is giving more like 8/-4, so I'm taking that as an improvement.
djembejim
Gizont N-Type have all tested good for me. Did get them months ago so maybe a different batch but they’ve all tested good on the vna..
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-02 5:20 p.m.

ｖｏｉｄ
10v/600mA peak during full sun, into a Adafruit bq25185 solar/li-on charger that is stepping voltage down to 3v for the Xiao. i think the maths should be okay, the weather is not. the battery seems to be sitting at 3.2v so i'll charge it up and see what happens
djembejim 2026-03-02 5:23 p.m.
Really 600ma! Would get a meter on it and have a butchers… Did you wire up to the battery pads underneath or to the top pins?
djembejim
Really 600ma! Would get a meter on it and have a butchers… Did you wire up to the battery pads underneath or to the top pins?
ｖｏｉｄ 2026-03-02 5:30 p.m.
both, i wired the power board 3v output to the 3v rail on the Xiao then ran another single + wire from the battery output on the power board to the batt+ pad on the xiao to correct the voltage reading in meshcore. stupid janky workaround that worked
ｖｏｉｄ
both, i wired the power board 3v output to the 3v rail on the Xiao then ran another single + wire from the battery output on the power board to the batt+ pad on the xiao to correct the voltage reading in meshcore. stupid janky workaround that worked

i shall, yeah this has been super reliable so far so i'll do some testing in the sunshine tomorrow

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-04 4:30 a.m.
Well it's much tighter than the SAW - with it I should hopefully be able to attenuate the other signals on 865-867, saw is wide open 863-877. The cavity from the factory is the same width-ish as the SAW, and the insertion loss is 1.3dB instead of 2.6dB for the SAW. So in stock form, yeah, SAW is far better value for money. Tuned, it could be... priceless?!
4:34 a.m.
Like you say getting some really decent 1/4l cans would be just the ticket. This little helical thing is compromise city

Hi @G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT , what's the crack with this observer firmware?
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-04 6:22 p.m.
I've got a bit of a hack going. WiFi FW. Running the MC_BOT Connects via TCP over my lan from an LXC. You can configure it to do 2x things, bot AND/OR report via MQTT to many servers.
GitHub - agessaman/meshcore-bot
Contribute to agessaman/meshcore-bot development by creating an account on GitHub.
6:23 p.m.
The Wigan Bot in #test is my mates wifi node. We have a VPN and his node connects back here and then bots to test, ping etc and reports to meshrank and analyzer

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-04 7:16 p.m.
On the meshrank site, Stuart has a link to generate a pub key to upload. Turn off the bot but telling it not to reply on on any channel - job done.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-04 7:18 p.m.
Excellent. I'll get into it!
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-04 7:19 p.m.
All you need to add for MR is this.... mqtt3_enabled = true mqtt3_server = meshrank.net mqtt3_port = 8883 mqtt3_use_tls = true mqtt3_tls_verify = true mqtt3_topic_packets = meshrank/uplink/xxxxxxxxxxxx/{PUBLIC_KEY}/packets
7:19 p.m.
Don't copy my key!
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-04 7:19 p.m.
Where xxx is the node and {PUBLIC_KEY} comes from the meshrank pubkey

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-05 2:29 p.m.
TL;DR; no idea! Had a look through IR-2030 and all I can come up with it's either something using the same conditions as us, "Non-specific short-range devices" IR2030/1/19, or something using IR2030/17 "Low duty cycle / high reliability device". Seems IR2030/29 smart meters are 870 – 874.4 or 873 - 875.6 (yeah we're short range better believe it)

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 6:42 a.m.
Top top for new players. Turn off auto add contacts.
6:44 a.m.
You only need to store contacts for local repeaters you want to manage/trace to and users you want to DM.
Exobyte 2026-03-07 6:44 a.m.
About to drive but I'll sort that when I stop.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 6:46 a.m.
If you're already upto the 350 limit. Once you've turned off auto add. purge contacts and start over. Use discover contacts to add the ones you want i.e. local repeaters you want to manage/trace to and users you want to DM. You don;t need any contacts to interact with the mesh.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
While having repeaters is a good idea, having so many in shuch close proximity just increases the collision rate exponentially and reduces the overall performance of the mesh.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 7:05 a.m.
Totally agree.. now up to 40% collisions here.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 7:07 a.m.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
I could be completely wrong here. I'm just applying knowledge gained from years of experience in general networking.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 7:11 a.m.

Exobyte 2026-03-07 7:45 a.m.
@G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 7:45 a.m.
untick all auto add
Luke Edwards 2026-03-07 8:05 a.m.
What’s the strategy then for too many close by repeaters? Coordination and removal?
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 8:11 a.m.
I dunno. It's an open decentralised network, nobody is in charge. I think people should ask themselves 1st off, does this repeater add value to the local mesh. Can I access the mesh using a companion rather than adding more hops and collisions. Not one size fits all use cases.
8:12 a.m.
Local group coordination I'm pretty sure would help a lot. I'm only aware of Jason and myself in the Solent area who are active in this forum.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 8:17 a.m.
Local coordination and education? For me rule #1 is don't tell people what to do.. I am guessing as things grind to a halt and go all meshtastic ppl will re-evealuate their repeaters.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-07 8:26 a.m.
I'm sure many new people will not know about things like collisions
1

Public key : fe63e711afac02bf4df518b175b96b52366f686bbf01e5f2b443cbdfaf51141a Hardware is not in final, elevated, location yet, so results may vary.

Luke Edwards 2026-03-07 2:23 p.m.

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
I'm sure many new people will not know about things like collisions
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-07 2:44 p.m.
Is this essentially a problem caused by too many repeaters in a given area?
RichKS (TacticalBacon)
Is this essentially a problem caused by too many repeaters in a given area?
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 4:16 p.m.
Yep, between Southampton and the IoW there have gotta be thirty-something repeaters at least so every flood turns into a cacophony.. It's pretty amazing how well it works considering!
mikebennett0612
New loft node added in KT24. Only on location for an hour or so - but has one Neighbour already with EG1 repeater. Hopefully might help fill out the mesh in and around that area!!!
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 4:16 p.m.
Awesome. We got another node up in Romsey the other day, now that area is properly covered - hoping to hear some mesh humans from that neck of the woods soon
1
Jason G5RKT IO90hw
Yep, between Southampton and the IoW there have gotta be thirty-something repeaters at least so every flood turns into a cacophony.. It's pretty amazing how well it works considering!
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-07 4:17 p.m.
So a case of physics being a harsh mistress.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 4:18 p.m.
And network "design" too!
4:19 p.m.
I have been trying to work out if the nodes / repeaters have CSMA/CD set up, i.e. listen before transmit - but we have created the ultimate hidden nodes problem
1
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-07 4:22 p.m.
Fair, I'm not a network specialist but I'm used to this all being limited by copper and subnets. Which Meshcore doesn't have.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 4:23 p.m.
We have the same problem with packet radio - although with that at least we have loads of channels and bands to choose from. This.. this is a more vicious species
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-07 4:37 p.m.
I think there's certainly an education piece here. One of the problems (I suspect) is that a lot of people like me get started with very little idea of how this works, and the first gear we buy is the recommended cheap stuff which works well enough but isn't exactly ideal.
4:39 p.m.
There seems not to be much nuance in the advice people get when starting out. I wouldn't recommend anyone start out with the stuff I bought for example (but using it and learning was really fun!).
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-07 4:46 p.m.
Well that's it, and the thing is once a new repeater goes up it completely changes the landscape - and there's been a lot of that recently! Until we got the box up at Redbridge I really couldn't MC at all from home, and now six weeks later it's a completely different animal - I can hit the IoW through your box, get into the mesh via Redbridge though Al's node.. completely different. And will continue to change frighteningly quickly no doubt!
4:46 p.m.
I guess as we're in the growing pains stage the idea has been to get as much up as possible, and it's really in the last few weeks that the mesh has started to get really grindy and wallowy. It's a learning experience for all of us fo' sho'
4:48 p.m.
There's gonna be a lot of cool new stuff we can do with scopes and longer ID bytes.. see where we go. If we could get everyone in this neck of the woods to set up their scope to scsc that would be a great start
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
Is it up and running for a test now ?
M0LIH - Ian - Gosport 2026-03-07 5:51 p.m.
Up and running yes, in a good location yet, no

Nice - I cant see it though Could you share the QR code?
1:29 p.m.

Luke Edwards 2026-03-08 1:36 p.m.

1:37 p.m.
1
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 6:15 p.m.
So - my musings on the new 1.14 firmware on the V4s. Having driven around this weekend as part of my dads taxi duties, in the same areas as previous, i think that the new firmware gives slightly better range (using https://mapme.sh/). This is obviously not very scientific, there are many variables (not least that my V4 repeater is in testing up a temp 10m pole at the moment) but to me - it feels better. Getting responses in hexes that i have visited over 10 times previous without response, and just generally finding more while out and about driving. I am still wondering if the update has had a play in the reduction of incoming messages to my repeater (gone from around 8/s to now around 2/sec - but i am not convinced on that yet, as the reduction was apparent before the update by about 12 hours (though this could be due to the repeater or other repeaters feeding me potentially being updated to 1.14) I have 2 SAW filters due for delivery this week (https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005007121506777.html?spm=a2g0o.order_list.order_list_main.4.5afa1802dSQGaa) - so will put them on both v4s and repeat and analyse. In short, I think its better, but no scientific proof, i have no background in RF (IT for the last 25 years), so take my ramblings with a pinch of salt. Still yet to see the UoS repeater which is a bit concerning, I would have expected to having LOS and around 9km distance. Cheers Meshers!!!
2
mikebennett0612
So - my musings on the new 1.14 firmware on the V4s. Having driven around this weekend as part of my dads taxi duties, in the same areas as previous, i think that the new firmware gives slightly better range (using https://mapme.sh/). This is obviously not very scientific, there are many variables (not least that my V4 repeater is in testing up a temp 10m pole at the moment) but to me - it feels better. Getting responses in hexes that i have visited over 10 times previous without response, and just generally finding more while out and about driving. I am still wondering if the update has had a play in the reduction of incoming messages to my repeater (gone from around 8/s to now around 2/sec - but i am not convinced on that yet, as the reduction was apparent before the update by about 12 hours (though this could be due to the repeater or other repeaters feeding me potentially being updated to 1.14) I have 2 SAW filters due for delivery this week (https://www.aliexpress.com/item/1005007121506777.html?spm=a2g0o.order_list.order_list_main.4.5afa1802dSQGaa) - so will put them on both v4s and repeat and analyse. In short, I think its better, but no scientific proof, i have no background in RF (IT for the last 25 years), so take my ramblings with a pinch of salt. Still yet to see the UoS repeater which is a bit concerning, I would have expected to having LOS and around 9km distance. Cheers Meshers!!!
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 6:36 p.m.
The onoly issue I see with the SAW is they are rated for max 20dBm, 100 mW. Your V4 put out 600.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
The onoly issue I see with the SAW is they are rated for max 20dBm, 100 mW. Your V4 put out 600.
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 6:38 p.m.
Yep - just for testing - ill set my TX to 13 (inc +7db takes to 20db). Seen any that can take the V4 max power anywhere?
mikebennett0612
Yep - just for testing - ill set my TX to 13 (inc +7db takes to 20db). Seen any that can take the V4 max power anywhere?
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 6:54 p.m.
I don't know if the filter will be damaged going over the max power rating. I'd suggest setting your TX to 10 to be on the safe side. What problem are youlooking to solve with the filter. Are you very close to a cell tower ? (edited)
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 6:57 p.m.
Thanks! Will go for 10 for testing. No problem to solve, just thought Id get some and try them to see if it helps increase my incoming messages - and the lottery win of getting a message out of base! (And still keep legal!)
6:58 p.m.
Local GSM towers around 600m away in both directions (5G)
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 7:00 p.m.
The filter is a last resort really. It will either help if you have lots of very close strong signals. If your out in the sticks away from any other transmitters it'll make the receiver worse, 3dB which is half of your power gone. The comprimose you'll have to make is wasting the V4's extra power output. (edited)
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 7:03 p.m.
Indeed. All in the name of testing! In a fairly dense area, noise floor goes from -95 to around -100 (though not sure if thats to do with the buck convertor in the housing next to the V4). Probably doesnt help that I have all manner of IT kit in the loft around 3m away from it all. Looking to put the scaffolding up in the next couple of weeks to put it on its final home for the next 5 years - so want to test all configurations!
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 7:06 p.m.
Good call - well worth testing so see with/without which works best. I don't have any filter at home but my steath repeater on the hill is about 200m away from a cell tower. In RF terms it's known as desense. Like hearing, if you're trying to listen to someone speaking but there is loud music.
7:07 p.m.
Did you see my site survey pictures of the RF spectrum on my local hill top site?
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 7:10 p.m.
Think I might have missed it - do you have the link? Ive just chucked my SDR on the 868 freq to see if anything odd going on - looks clear to my (unducated RF) self....
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 7:10 p.m.
7:11 p.m.
The little grey line in the meddle is 869.618 and the tiny red pixels is some LoRa traffic, (edited)
mikebennett0612 2026-03-08 7:11 p.m.
Im running DSD+ so smaller spectrum....
7:13 p.m.
Been running DSD+ since probably 2013.... good but of software for digital listening i think1
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-08 7:14 p.m.
Yes, I know DSD. The fast lane version is now free.

Seeed wonder parcel on its way... got 3 x ESP32 and 3 x Nrf boards... and a load of govee sensors to play around with. Thinking of buiding a test tree node for my 40m tree..... im sure i can get out with that!

Luke Edwards
Yes I think it does because it thinks the message is outside of a time it needs repeating iirc when I was reading about clock drift
Bryan#1364 (CATM) 2026-03-12 6:56 a.m.
Clock doesn't affect repeating, just affects adverts if ever the clock has been correct then earlier adverts will just be ignored but doesn't affect any messaging

ever since i did a hash update because of the collisions ...that thing hasn't been right, the last thing i did when i saw it was a clock sync but i guess its not holding it very well, it also doesn't advert when it should. seems to be once every 3 days which concerns me because i use the internet to montitor whether its still alive or not. when it adverts, it seems to hit some far away nodes (see below pic) previously it was haphazarly stashed in a tree at 45* until i cable tied it upright, maybe that has something to do with it? i'm staying in ealing at the mo but i might just run my laptop on the passenger seat on the way home with my V4 and do some mapme,sh logging for the giggles (edited)

RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-17 9:02 a.m.

9:03 a.m.
Meshcore on Cardputer ADV is a thing.
1
Luke Edwards 2026-03-17 10:06 a.m.
I’m not jealous
10:07 a.m.
That’s super cool. I was looking at the kits on pihut and aliexpress
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-17 10:10 a.m.
The Cardputer itself is a pretty neat device, Meshcore on it has some fairly obvious limitations and frustrations.
10:16 a.m.
Like, the really tiny screen is also pretty low res, it's not ideal for reading text on. The keyboard isn't the worst, but it's not ideal for tying more than a word or two.
10:18 a.m.
You CAN at least pair it via Bluetooth and use the app, which solves both of those problems but kinda ignores the fact that it's got the screen and keyboard which is the USP of something like this.
10:20 a.m.
And the ESP32S3 processor is fairly power hungry, the battery generally lasts 6 hours or so, but that's not enough for an all day meshcore carry.
10:20 a.m.
So basically, it's pretty cool, not actually useful compared to other devices.
10:23 a.m.
Also, stores the config locally. Not on SD. Means it loses everything when you use M5Launcher to switch firmwares.
Luke Edwards 2026-03-17 2:55 p.m.
You’ve saved me some money from the sound of it!
RichKS (TacticalBacon)
So basically, it's pretty cool, not actually useful compared to other devices.
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-17 3:48 p.m.
Pretty cool, not very useful.. sounds like my kinda jam
2

pir 2026-03-18 7:15 a.m.
I have one of those, it's my standard wandering around radio. Other than the use of RP-SMA (which can be fixed with a new pigtail) I do like the physical power switch and so on.
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-18 7:17 a.m.
Yeah, I looked at all the available options for pre-built companions and while the T1000 I've been using is super convenient for carrying that tiny internal antenna isn't great once you're away from your repeater.
7:22 a.m.
There's basically this (with its daft-but-replacable RP connector) or the T-Echo (which is a pretty old device and significantly more expensive).
pir 2026-03-18 7:26 a.m.
I bought a couple of the Thinknode M1s thinking they looked nice and small, when I didn't know much about how things worked. Also RP-SMA and soldered to the board. I don't think the eink screens work well with the code and just not that great devices. Not awful if you're in reasonable range and using them over bluetooth though.
RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-18 7:31 a.m.
I read a review of the M5 (I think) where the reviewer pointed out that the e ink screen should provide a long battery life, but the ESP32S3 processor in them made that irrelevant.
Luke Edwards 2026-03-18 7:49 a.m.

pir 2026-03-18 8:01 a.m.

RichKS (TacticalBacon) 2026-03-18 8:07 a.m.

8:11 a.m.

Rob 2026-03-21 1:37 p.m.
Butser (93) has repeats turned off, as the new Butser (7e) has been put up and is very close to it. 7e appears to perform better so my next visit will likely be to update 7e firmware and take 93 down.
2
Rob
Butser (93) has repeats turned off, as the new Butser (7e) has been put up and is very close to it. 7e appears to perform better so my next visit will likely be to update 7e firmware and take 93 down.
G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT 2026-03-21 2:08 p.m.
Hi Rob, what happended to the Butser about 2 revisions ago ?
mking834 2026-03-21 2:37 p.m.
I have set my Seed sensecap solar node up, not yet put it in it's final position, but it seems to be working. When I look at my neighbours on meshrank, some are set to "Mutual" others to "Incoming only" which is what mine is set to, do I need to change this.
Rob 2026-03-21 5:17 p.m.
So.. The original trial Butser 93 was replaced by 7e with a fake alpha antenna but you & I felt it performed worse. I went back to screw on a mcgill instead and made a mess of the case so threw 93 back up with shrink wrap to try and waterproof the antenna a little. It's still there now but repeats turned off. 7e was rebuilt with a 3dbi mcgill and placed about 8m away, found the best spot that I knew could receive RG17. I plan to retrieve 93 and just leave 7e in place. (edited)
mking834
I have set my Seed sensecap solar node up, not yet put it in it's final position, but it seems to be working. When I look at my neighbours on meshrank, some are set to "Mutual" others to "Incoming only" which is what mine is set to, do I need to change this.
jburgess777 2026-03-21 5:52 p.m.
I don’t think it is a setting but is derived from the observed message paths
  • Incoming only = you received messages from them
  • mutual = you received messages from them, and they received message from you
mking834 2026-03-22 10:21 a.m.
thanks, I am new to this, and couldn't find the answer anywhere (edited)

Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-24 3:49 a.m.
Morning folks Regarding the adverts, most mesh traffic is flood ads and in busy areas it's starting to cripple the network, so I the recommendation is that less is more - I have our boxes set to 72 hours. Zero hop ads are more useful for local users and don't congest the mesh, so 12 hours seems appropriate.
1
3:51 a.m.

mikebennett0612 2026-03-25 2:31 p.m.
Wismesh RAK 1w booster board has arrived and I have assembled it ready to go up the flagpole when the wind dies down a bit. Currently testing it on my single story flat roof extension at TX 10. Hopeing to hear more, and maybe finally bust out onto the main mesh when playing around with the higher power settings (testing only! )
Colz
Hmm makes me wonder if the wildcard overrides them (edited)
Jason G5RKT IO90hw 2026-03-25 2:57 p.m.
That wild card is just the rule to handle scope-less packets. Without any scopes set up then all packets are repeated regardless of scope by a repeater, once you start adding scopes then scope-less packets, and packets with a defined scope, are passed and everything else is dropped.
mikebennett0612
Wismesh RAK 1w booster board has arrived and I have assembled it ready to go up the flagpole when the wind dies down a bit. Currently testing it on my single story flat roof extension at TX 10. Hopeing to hear more, and maybe finally bust out onto the main mesh when playing around with the higher power settings (testing only! )

typhoon_9978 2026-04-10 8:55 a.m.
Hi all! I'm very new to meshcore, you might have seen me in #public and #test. Thank you if you've replied and apologies for the wall of text! I have become instantly addicted and I'm absolutely clueless I'm learning as I go, building a repeater (currently HobbitonV4), gradually converting my free money into radio equipment . I'm a bit stuck as I'm out on the edge a bit and I could do with some help. My only reliable gateway to the wider mesh is "so'ton west omni", which I reckon is true for the area. It's most likely getting spammed to bits. What I'd like to do is increase the available paths onto the mesh, and improve things in the New Forest direction if I can. Is it as "easy" as taking a see(ee)eeed P1-pro up to Tatchbury Mount somewhere? I'd rather coordinate with others if there are better ways of doing it... Very happy to RTFM, if people can point me in the direction of a FM that's half decent, there's a lot I don't know. I don't even know what my questions are!

G7RPG - Peter - IO90LT
Hi There, I've not been meshing much recently but just getting back into it. Are you looking at deploying something on a high point out in the wild? (edited)
typhoon_9978 2026-04-10 1:50 p.m.
Aye, somewhere, somehow. I'd like to do something directional towards Salisbury to try and create a second route North from this edge of the New Forest.
typhoon_9978 2026-04-11 8:05 a.m.
Interesting morning. I took a 2db stick antenna up Tatchbury Mount. The goal being to see if I can find a northern route out of the western side of the solent. Pretty much all traffic hit either my repeaters I have at home, soton west omni or it started picking up IoW repeaters. to me this didn't really count as often the IoW repeaters are second hops to soton west omni, also, it's not north so its not the goal. The North side of Tatchbury Mount was dark. Unfortunately something of the right size to get further north to Romsey would be hard to hide and expensive to lose. We could probably do with something in the Broadlands Estate or Ower Macdonalds or Hayes Music. It'd be grand to put something there that can be maintained.
typhoon_9978

djembejim 2026-04-11 12:05 p.m.
Something to consider. IOW also has a route north by Buster that hits my repeater directly in Wiltshire. IOW to near Marlborough is 4 hops and pretty solid. The south of Salisbury repeater Stand Lynch Hill hits Morgan’s hill repeater to the north so there are numerous routes south to north. more is better though
typhoon_9978 2026-04-11 12:20 p.m.
Yeah we need more routes north. Here is a terrible map. Everything from the West of Southampton comes through Soton W, then to IoW, then back up to Butser. Which isn't bad, but its just one route.
typhoon_9978

djembejim 2026-04-11 1:03 p.m.
One thing I’d say about repeaters is make them as road accessible as possible. When there’s a hike and a couple of hours round trip to update or do maintenance they become a bit of a ballache. If they really have to be out in the boonies, then a solid solar battery setup is key. @IanT [IT-Orp-S omni] has a great pcb that has a supervisor IC onboard so no more brown out hikes to go and reboot stuff
IanT [IT-Orp-S omni] 2026-04-11 1:16 p.m.
Yeah, I don't believe in failure.
1:20 p.m.
I've never sold anything like that before, but I'm happy to make them available to anyone who wants one. My first off is currently under test; it has so far run flat and recovered itself from solar, also succeeded in my bench testing. The only observation that isn't quite as I'd like it is the DW01 chip on the TP4056 module is a little over-protective of the battery and cuts off at 3V, so there would be a little more emergency energy there (I was happy to let it go down to 2.8V).
1
1:20 p.m.
1:21 p.m.
1:21 p.m.
Some surface mount parts hidden on the back of the board, but otherwise really easy to build.
djembejim 2026-04-11 2:22 p.m.
With the supervisor IC and Nrf board it’s a solid set and forget solution for remote repeaters
2:23 p.m.
What do you use to report the ina219 current telemetry again?
djembejim
What do you use to report the ina219 current telemetry again?
IanT [IT-Orp-S omni] 2026-04-11 3:15 p.m.
I rolled my own firmware for that... 1) WiFi connected node that logs into the repeater (it can cope with 1-2 hops, more if the mesh is reliable) and links to mqtt 2) mqtt server (broker) on a machine running NodeRed, which takes the data and squirts it out to ThingSpeak. The Web panels are a combination of NodeRed (gauges) and Thingspeak (plots).
3:16 p.m.
But as you know you can just login to the repeater and the data is visible as channel 2
Luke Edwards 2026-04-11 4:09 p.m.
Latest setup. Nice base unit
1
IanT [IT-Orp-S omni] 2026-04-11 6:17 p.m.
Nice
